objective: Test an interior mixed-hook source family for the rpf50 exact-support blocker and record whether it solves the Wave 55/56 source-capacity shortfall.
non_negotiables: The interior source is diagnostic-only unless it reaches rpf50 support and later passes exact materialization, replay, split, projection, and baseline gates.
Capacity-only combined variants must not be treated as selected exact support.
Pattern-limit truncation must be recorded when an interior-first combined source reduces prior mixed-hook supply.
No model or decomposition-benefit claim can use this source report directly.

tasks.prompt: Add and run a diagnostic interior mixed-hook source report at rows-per-family 50.
acceptance: Standalone interior source selected support is recorded.
Combined interior-first variants are clearly marked capacity-only.
The report records whether rpf50 reaches the 150-row target.

tasks.prompt: Record Wave 59 as a source-family no-go and keep rpf50 exact-support claims blocked.
commands: python3 -m py_compile engine/ml_model.py engine/test.py agents/network.py agents/label_schema.py
acceptance: Docs state that standalone interior hooks select only 11 rows at rpf50.
Docs state that interior-first combined capacity is 81 rows, below the 150-row target.
Docs state that pattern-limit ordering can reduce prior mixed-hook supply.
Docs route next work away from interior-first source ordering.

integration_gates.command: python3 agents/network.py validate && python3 agents/network.py validate-wave agents/waves/wave_59_interior_mixed_hook_source_no_go.json
integration_gates.success_signal: The Wave 59 plan is internally consistent.
The Wave 59 source report is valid JSON.
